mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.2 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning as investors price in higher recession probabilities, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and real personal savings rates signals a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance in a rolling regression, or the combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production relative to GDP growth indicates supply-side resilience that supports corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate exceeds GDPC1 growth rate for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The spread between industrial production and GDP growth shows no predictive power for forward returns, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings rates reflect increased household liquidity buffers that sustain consumer spending during economic uncertainty, directly supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with savings rate changes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, tight monetary conditions increase borrowing costs for leveraged corporations, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of observations, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market breakdown that accelerates recession pricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform a single-series baseline in out-of-sample directional accuracy, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in quarterly GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: The GDP growth threshold fails to predict negative returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the forward return distribution shows no skew toward negative values.
